title: Quick Start
text: INLAocc fits occupancy models — kind estimate species occurrence accounting imperfect detection — using INLA instead MCMC. ’ve used spOccupancy, data format model types . difference speed: seconds instead minutes.

title: Quick Start
text: Simulate data fit single-species occupancy model: formula interface occu(occ.formula, det.formula, data) — occupancy covariates left, detection covariates right.
code: library(INLAocc)  sim <- simulate_occu(N = 200, J = 4, seed = 42) fit <- occu(~ occ_x1 + occ_x2, ~ det_x1, data = sim$data) summary(fit)

title: Quick Start
text: INLAocc parses mixed-model random effects via AST walker: Supported syntax: (1 | group), (x | group), (x || group) (uncorrelated), (1 | /b) (nested).
code: # Add a regional random intercept sim$data$occ.covs$region <- sample(1:5, 200, replace = TRUE)  fit_re <- occu(   ~ occ_x1 + (1 | region),   ~ det_x1,   data = sim$data ) ranef(fit_re)

title: Quick Start
text: Pass coordinates enable SPDE spatial random effect:
code: sim_sp <- simulate_occu(N = 200, J = 4, spatial_range = 0.2, seed = 123)  fit_sp <- occu(~ occ_x1, ~ det_x1, data = sim_sp$data,                spatial = sim_sp$data$coords) summary(fit_sp)

title: Quick Start
text: produces 2x2 panel: QQ plot PIT residuals, residuals vs fitted, dispersion test, Moran’s correlogram (coordinates available).
code: checkModel(fit_sp)

title: Quick Start
text: diagnostic also available standalone function: return htest objects, $p.value print() work expected.
code: testUniformity(fit)     # KS test: are PIT residuals uniform? testDispersion(fit)     # over/underdispersion testOutliers(fit)       # simulation envelope testZeroInflation(fit)  # excess unoccupied sites moranI(fit_sp)          # spatial autocorrelation in residuals

title: Quick Start
text: DHARMa installed, one call gives full DHARMa object:
code: res <- dharma(fit) DHARMa::testSpatialAutocorrelation(res,   x = sim_sp$data$coords[, 1],   y = sim_sp$data$coords[, 2])

title: Quick Start
text: compare_models() supports "aic", "bic", "waic" criteria.
code: m1 <- occu(~ 1, ~ 1, data = sim$data) m2 <- occu(~ occ_x1, ~ det_x1, data = sim$data) m3 <- occu(~ occ_x1 + occ_x2, ~ det_x1, data = sim$data)  # Compare with AIC weights compare_models(null = m1, elev = m2, full = m3, criterion = "aic")  # Model-averaged predictions (Burnham & Anderson) avg <- modelAverage(null = m1, elev = m2, full = m3, criterion = "aic") avg$psi_hat   # weighted-average occupancy avg$psi_se    # unconditional standard errors

title: Quick Start
code: # ggpredict-style: vary one covariate, hold others at mean predict(fit, terms = "occ_x1 [-2:2 by=0.5]")  # Design-matrix prediction at new sites predict(fit, X.0 = data.frame(occ_x1 = 0, occ_x2 = 1))  # Marginal effect plot data marginal_effect(fit, "occ_x1")

title: Quick Start
code: sim_ms <- simMsOcc(N = 100, J = 3, n_species = 10, seed = 200)  fit_ms <- occu(~ occ_x1, ~ det_x1, data = sim_ms$data, multispecies = TRUE) summary(fit_ms)  # Site-level species richness richness(fit_ms)

title: Quick Start
code: sim_t <- simTOcc(N = 50, J = 3, n_seasons = 5, seed = 300)  fit_t <- occu(~ 1, ~ 1, data = sim_t$data, temporal = "ar1") summary(fit_t)

title: INLAocc: Occupancy Models via INLA
text: Occupancy models via INLA — fast alternative MCMC. Fit single-species, multi-species, spatial, temporal, integrated occupancy models one function. INLAocc uses EM algorithm INLA M-step, giving fast accurate estimates species occurrence accounting imperfect detection.

title: INLAocc: Occupancy Models via INLA
code: library(INLAocc)  sim <- simulate_occu(N = 200, J = 4, seed = 42) fit <- occu(~ occ_x1 + occ_x2, ~ det_x1, data = sim$data) summary(fit)  # One-call diagnostic panel checkModel(fit)

title: INLAocc: Occupancy Models via INLA
text: Occupancy models separate two entangled processes — species actually occurs (occupancy) happen detect (detection). Getting right matters conservation decisions, fitting models MCMC can slow, especially spatial multi-species extensions. INLAocc uses EM algorithm INLA M-step, multiple imputation step corrects attenuation bias introduces. estimates correlate > 0.99 MCMC, fraction runtime.

title: INLAocc: Occupancy Models via INLA
text: One occu() call handles everything — model type determined arguments pass:

title: INLAocc: Occupancy Models via INLA
text: Mixed-model formula syntax native AST parsing:
code: occu(~ elev + (1 | region), ~ effort, data)              # random intercept occu(~ elev + (1 + elev | region), ~ effort, data)       # random slope occu(~ elev + (elev || region), ~ effort, data)           # uncorrelated occu(~ elev + (1 | site/plot), ~ effort, data)            # nested

title: INLAocc: Occupancy Models via INLA
text: Full diagnostic suite zero external dependencies: DHARMa installed, dharma(fit) creates full DHARMa object.
code: # Simulation-based residual checks simulate(fit, nsim = 250)          # posterior predictive simulation pitResiduals(fit)                  # PIT scaled residuals testUniformity(fit)                # KS test on PIT residuals testDispersion(fit)                # over/underdispersion testOutliers(fit)                  # simulation envelope test testZeroInflation(fit)             # excess zeros  # Spatial / temporal autocorrelation moranI(fit)                        # Moran's I (inverse-distance or k-NN) durbinWatson(fit)                  # Durbin-Watson for temporal models variogram(fit)                     # empirical semivariogram  # GOF and model comparison ppcOccu(fit)                       # posterior predictive checks waicOccu(fit)                      # WAIC AIC(fit); BIC(fit)                 # information criteria  # One-call diagnostic panel checkModel(fit)                    # QQ, residuals, dispersion, correlogram

title: spOccupancy → INLAocc Compatibility Matrix
text: spOccupancy returns full MCMC sample matrices every parameter. INLAocc returns point estimates INLA summary statistics. affects: - Every $*.samples field (20+ fields across model types) - fitted() return values - residuals() return values - getSVCSamples() return values - predict() partially (psi.0 samples, z.0 p.0 don’t) Fix: Add $.occu_inla accessor lazily generates samples via INLA::inla.posterior.sample() $beta.samples, $alpha.samples, etc. accessed. provides backwards compatibility without pre-computing unused samples.

what: INLAocc: Occupancy Models via Integrated Nested Laplace Approximation — INLAocc-package
title: INLAocc: Occupancy Models via Integrated Nested Laplace Approximation — INLAocc-package
text: Fits single-species, multi-species, spatial, temporal, integrated, spatially-varying coefficient occupancy models using Integrated Nested Laplace Approximations (INLA). Provides fast alternative MCMC-based frameworks like 'spOccupancy', compatible API including mixed-model random effects syntax, posterior predictive checks, WAIC, k-fold cross-validation, design-matrix prediction. Uses EM algorithm INLA M-step two-process (occupancy/detection) likelihood.

Enables spatial SPDE models. temporal "ar1" "iid" multi-season models. Requires data$y list detection matrices. n.factors Integer number latent factors. Requires spatial. spde.args List arguments passed occu_spatial() spatial coordinate matrix. priors occu_priors() object spOccupancy-compatible named list. occ.re Explicit list occu_re() specs occupancy. det.re Explicit list occu_re() specs detection. max.iter Maximum EM iterations (default 50). tol Convergence tolerance (default 1e-4). damping EM damping factor 0–1 (default 0.3). ensemble Logical; TRUE, use ensemble averaging across multiple imputation chains (default FALSE). num.threads Thread specification INLA, ":B" outer B inner OpenMP threads (default "1:1"). k.fold Number cross-validation folds (default 0, CV). verbose 0 = silent, 1 = iteration summaries, 2 = full INLA output.

what: Stochastic EM (VB E-step replacement) ✗ — cut after testing
title: INLAocc — Implemented Improvements
text: Attempted: sampling hard z’s Bernoulli(w) EM iteration. Failed due absorbing state: extreme psi estimates → near-zero sampling weights → z=0 → extreme psi. occupancy model’s two-process structure makes within-EM stochastic sampling unstable. MI debiasing post-convergence remains correct approach — uses hard-z sampling converged weights, avoiding absorbing state.

title: INLAocc — Implemented Improvements
text: occu_areal(adj, model = "bym2") creates areal spatial effects adjacency matrices, spdep::nb objects, INLA graph files. Alternative SPDE grid/administrative unit data.

title: INLAocc — Implemented Improvements
text: resolve_group_vals() handles interaction groups (site:year) constructing factors component columns. Formula syntax (1|site) + (1|observer) already worked crossed via independent f() terms; nested (1|/b) now correctly resolves.

title: INLAocc — Implemented Improvements
text: MI now runs K=20 (configurable) stops early -imputation variance stabilizes (relative change < 5%). Minimum K=5 checking. Saves 30-50% MI compute well-identified models.

title: INLAocc — Implemented Improvements
text: engine_ms now uses parallel::parLapply options(INLAocc.cores = N) set, matching engine_ms_temporal’s parallelism. Also cleaned hardcoded devtools path temporal engine.

title: INLAocc — Implemented Improvements
text: occu(..., ensemble = TRUE) uses pool_community_ensemble(): fits community estimates B=50 random species subsets (70% ), pools across subsets robustness outlier species.

title: INLAocc — Implemented Improvements
text: occu(..., num.threads = "2:4") passes INLA calls. Default remains "1:1" reproducibility.

title: INLAocc — Implemented Improvements
text: Added test-engines-smoke.R tests : engine_ss, stochastic EM, engine_ss_spatial, engine_temporal, engine_int, engine_ms, ensemble pooling, engine_ms_lf, engine_jsdm_lf, formula parser edge cases, occu_areal constructor, pool_community_ensemble, resolve_group_vals. Coverage went ~5% ~25%.

title: INLAocc — Implemented Improvements
text: SVC engine: occ_re_svc_spatial_* columns attached spatial stack data RMSE/beta tolerance tests occasionally fail due EM convergence variance small datasets

title: INLAocc 0.1.0
text: Initial release.

title: INLAocc 0.1.0
text: EM-INLA algorithm scaled-binomial occupancy M-step. Novel EM + multiple imputation hybrid beta debiasing (cor > 0.99 MCMC). Adaptive damping GLM warm start convergence stability. Unified occu() API dispatching model types.

title: INLAocc 0.1.0
text: Single-species: occu(), occu_inla(). Spatial SPDE: occu(..., spatial = coords), spatial_occu_inla(). Multi-season AR(1): occu(..., temporal = "ar1"), temporal_occu_inla(). Multi-species community: occu(..., multispecies = TRUE), ms_occu_inla(). Latent factor multi-species: lfMsOccu_inla(), sfMsOccu_inla(). Spatially-varying coefficients: svcOccu_inla(). Integrated multi-source: intOccu_inla(), spIntOccu_inla(). Joint SDM (detection): lfJSDM_inla().

title: INLAocc 0.1.0
text: Native AST-based formula parser (1 | group), (x | group), (x || group) syntax. Supports nested grouping (1 | /b), operator expansion (*b | group). Pure AST walker — regex deparsed code.

title: INLAocc 0.1.0
text: ppcOccu(): posterior predictive checks (Freeman-Tukey, chi-squared). waicOccu(): WAIC model comparison (per-species .sp). fitted(), residuals(): S3 methods model types. simulate(): posterior predictive simulation (site-level observation-level). pitResiduals(): PIT scaled residuals integer randomisation. testUniformity(): KS test PIT residuals. testDispersion(): simulation-based /underdispersion test. testOutliers(): simulation envelope exceedance test. testZeroInflation(): observed vs expected zero-detection sites. moranI(): Moran’s inverse-distance (default) k-NN weights. durbinWatson(): Durbin-Watson test temporal autocorrelation. variogram(): empirical semivariogram plot() method. checkModel(): one-call 2x2 diagnostic panel (QQ, residuals, dispersion, correlogram). dharma(): optional DHARMa convenience bridge.

title: INLAocc 0.1.0
text: compare_models(): AIC, BIC, WAIC delta Akaike/Schwarz weights. modelAverage(): Burnham & Anderson full model averaging unconditional SEs. AIC(), BIC() via logLik() correct df nobs attributes.

title: INLAocc 0.1.0
text: coef(), confint(), vcov(), nobs(), logLik(). update() formula modification support. tidy(), glance(): broom-compatible output. ranef(): random effect posterior summaries. summary(), print(), plot() model types.

title: INLAocc 0.1.0
text: predict() ggpredict-style terms argument. Design-matrix prediction via X.0, X.p.0, coords.0. predict_spatial(): convenience wrapper spatial interpolation. marginal_effect(): single-covariate response curves. richness(): site-level species richness multi-species models.
